content.problemStatement:
**This is the hard version of the problem. Here you need to find the sum of f(S)f(S)f(S) over all 2N2^N2N subsets SSS  modulo 998244353998244353998244353.**

For a tree TTT consisting of NNN labelled nodes, labelled 111 through NNN; consider having 222 markers, marker AAA initially located at node 111 and marker BBB located at node NNN.

In one move, you can move **exactly** one of the 222 markers from it's current node to an adjacent node. A node is considered visited if there was at least one time instant where **either marker** was placed on the node.

For a given subset SSS of vertices, define f(S)f(S)f(S) as the minimum moves required to make all vertices in the set SSS visited, such that the markers are back at the starting nodes (i.e. marker AAA is back at node 111 and marker BBB at node NNN). The vertices not present in SSS may be visited or not visited.

For example, for the empty set S={}S = \{\}S={} or the set S={1,N}S = \{1, N\}S={1,N}, f(S)f(S)f(S) is always 000, because the empty set has no vertices to visit, and 111 and NNN are considered visited at the very start itself.

You are given a tree consisting of NNN labelled nodes. Find the sum of f(S)f(S)f(S) over all 2N2^N2N possible subsets SSS. Since the answer may be large, print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

The tree is given in the form of par2,par3,…,parN\text{par}_2, \text{par}_3, \ldots, \text{par}_Npar2​,par3​,…,parN​ such that 1≤pari<i1 \le \text{par}_i < i1≤pari​<i, and there is an edge between (pari,i)(\text{par}_i, i)(pari​,i) for each 2≤i≤N2 \le i \le N2≤i≤N.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN - the size of the tree.
The second line contains N−1N - 1N−1 integers, par2,par3,…,parN\text{par}_2, \text{par}_3, \ldots, \text{par}_Npar2​,par3​,…,parN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of f(S)f(S)f(S) over all 2N2^N2N subsets SSS modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 500050005000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: All subsets have f(S)=0f(S) = 0f(S)=0.
Test case 222: f(S)=2f(S) = 2f(S)=2 if and only if 2∈S2 \in S2∈S, otherwise f(S)=0f(S) = 0f(S)=0. There are 444 subsets including node 222, hence the answer is 888.
